_comment_purpose: Placeholder skeleton for Sentry dashboard 'Overview'. The exact shape Sentry's API expects on import is documented at https://docs.sentry.io/api/discover/ — that surface evolves so this file may need editing before import. Treat it as a starting artifact ops can iterate on, not a contract. The source of truth for widget intent is docs/observability/SENTRY_DASHBOARDS.md §2.
title: Overview

widgets._comment: W1.1 — Error rate por módulo. 4-value bounded enum from sentryInstrumentation.ts:42-46. Adjust series shape to whatever Sentry Discover currently expects.
widgets.title: Error rate por módulo

widgets._comment: W1.4 — Lighthouse scores. PLACEHOLDER: depends on Lighthouse → Sentry pipeline (Brecha D, ETA Sprint 21+). If no measurements yet, the widget will show 'No data'. Replace 'measurements.lh_*' with the real measurement keys once the pipeline is wired.
widgets.title: Lighthouse scores trend

widgets._comment: W1.5 — Fatal events (24h). Threshold visual in UI: red if > 0, gray if = 0.
widgets.title: Fatal events (24h)

_comment_projects: Leave empty so the dashboard inherits the active project filter, or fill with explicit project IDs once known.
_comment_import_notes: 2. If Sentry's UI supports JSON import on the create modal, paste this file (after editing the conditions to your environment values).
3. If not, recreate each widget manually using the queries above as the source of truth.
4. Validate against the Discover API: https://docs.sentry.io/api/discover/.
5. After import, save the dashboard with the exact title 'Overview' so the cross-references in SENTRY_DASHBOARDS.md still resolve.
